title: Final Market Surveillance Committee Opinion - Extended Day-Ahead Market Congestion Revenue Allocation - Jun 16, 2025
relevance_reason: EDAM congestion revenue allocation mechanisms directly affect how hydropower and pumped-storage resources recover transmission costs and hedge congestion exposure through firm transmission rights, which is critical to their market participation and financial viability.
stakeholders: Benjamin F. Hobbs (Market Surveillance Committee of the California ISO); James Bushnell (Market Surveillance Committee of the California ISO); Scott M. Harvey (Market Surveillance Committee of the California ISO)
ai_summary: The MSC opinion addresses CAISO's proposal for allocating congestion revenues from parallel flows in EDAM, which affects how BAAs with hydropower resources can maintain firm transmission rights (OATT/NITS) to hedge congestion costs and recover fixed network costs. Year 1 focuses on balanced firm point-to-point and NITS rights with self-scheduled resources; future years may expand to economically-scheduled transactions, materially impacting how hydro generators and PSH facilities can participate in multi-BAA markets.

title: MSC Final Opinion - Rules for Bidding Above the Soft Offer Cap - Price Formation Enhancements - May 2024
relevance_reason: This MSC opinion directly addresses FERC Order 831 offer cap rules for both battery storage and hydropower resources, analyzing pricing and bidding mechanisms that affect how energy-limited hydro resources manage opportunity costs and dispatch timing.
stakeholders: Benjamin F. Hobbs (Market Surveillance Committee of the California ISO); James Bushnell (Market Surveillance Committee of the California ISO); Scott M. Harvey (Market Surveillance Committee of the California ISO)
ai_summary: The document evaluates CAISO's proposed changes to soft offer cap rules for summer 2024 affecting both battery and hydropower resources under Order 831. Key issues include preventing premature discharge of energy-limited resources, improving real-time pricing to reflect opportunity costs, and addressing scheduling inefficiencies that have caused dispatch problems over 2022-2024, with particular concern about low hydro years reducing supply margins.

title: MSC Final Opinion on Extended Day-Ahead Market
relevance_reason: EDAM's Resource Sufficiency Evaluation, transmission optimization, and market design directly affect hydropower dispatch, resource adequacy requirements, and participation in multi-balancing-area markets.
stakeholders: Benjamin F. Hobbs (California ISO Market Surveillance Committee); James Bushnell (California ISO Market Surveillance Committee); Scott M. Harvey (California ISO Market Surveillance Committee)
ai_summary: The MSC opinion addresses EDAM's RSE framework, inter-BAA transmission management, and GHG accounting—all critical for hydropower scheduling and cost recovery across western balancing areas. The document highlights unresolved details on supply shortfall allocation and export constraints that will significantly impact how hydro resources are committed and compensated in the proposed day-ahead market.

title: Market Surveillance Committee Final Opinion on Day-Ahead Market Enhancements

title: Presentation - Congestion Cost Analysis from Historical WEIM Performance - Mar 06, 2026
relevance_reason: The document analyzes congestion revenue allocation (CRA) methodology impacts on transmission rights and self-scheduling in EDAM, which directly affects how hydropower resources—particularly PacifiCorp's internal generation and inter-area transfers—will be compensated and dispatched in the Extended Day Ahead Market.
ai_summary: This analysis examines how revised CRA methodology will allocate congestion rents between CAISO and PacifiCorp areas under EDAM, with specific focus on incentives for exercising transmission rights and self-scheduling internal generation. The findings indicate PacifiCorp could receive $1.6–$2.5 million monthly in congestion incentives, which is material for hydropower operators' participation strategy in EDAM and affects the economic viability of inter-area hydro transfers.

title: Presentation - Extended Day-Ahead Market (EDAM) Configurable Parameters - Feb 06, 2026
relevance_reason: The document analyzes Extended Day-Ahead Market (EDAM) configurable parameters with significant implications for energy storage, battery resources, and imbalance reserve procurement—all directly affecting hydropower and pumped-storage dispatch and market competitiveness.
ai_summary: CAISO is evaluating five key EDAM market parameters (envelope constraint multiplier, IR bid price cap, default availability bids, deployed imbalance reserve factor, and enforced transmission constraints) through sensitivity analyses. Battery envelope constraint multipliers and DIR factors show inverse correlations affecting storage resource procurement and IR pricing, with direct implications for how PSH and flexible hydro resources compete in imbalance reserve markets and transmission-constrained scenarios.

title: Presentation - Extended Day-Ahead Market (EDAM) Congestion Charge Allocation Impacts - Mar 06, 2026
relevance_reason: The document analyzes EDAM congestion charge allocation design impacts on PacifiCorp's transmission scheduling, which directly affects hydropower resources' cost of service and market participation through transmission-dependent power flows.

ai_summary: This presentation examines how CAISO's Extended Day-Ahead Market (EDAM) congestion settlement design changes impact PacifiCorp's East-to-West transactions compared to Western EIM base schedules. The analysis is relevant to hydropower operators because PacifiCorp is a major hydropower producer, and congestion charge allocation directly affects the economics of scheduling power across the transmission system—a key consideration for hydro plants' participation in day-ahead markets and their revenue recovery.

title: Presentation - Extended Day-Ahead Market (EDAM) Congestion Revenue Allocation - Mar 06, 2026
relevance_reason: EDAM congestion revenue allocation design directly affects how hydropower and storage resources recover transmission congestion revenues and manage self-scheduling incentives, which influence their market participation and dispatch economics.
ai_summary: This presentation addresses Phase 2 design of EDAM congestion revenue allocation, including evaluation of negotiated entitlements and parallel flow allocation mechanisms. These mechanisms affect how hydropower and pumped-storage operators recover congestion revenues, manage transmission rights, and respond to self-scheduling incentives—key economic factors in their market bidding strategies and operational flexibility provision.

title: Presentation - Extended Day-Ahead Market (EDAM) Imbalance Reserve Costs Market Surveillance Committee - Feb 06, 2026
relevance_reason: The document directly addresses market design and modeling choices affecting imbalance reserves, storage resource participation costs, and commitment requirements in EDAM—all central to how hydropower and pumped-storage facilities will compete and be valued in the extended day-ahead market.
stakeholders: Scott Harvey (California ISO / Market Surveillance Committee)
ai_summary: Scott Harvey's analysis identifies critical EDAM market design features that significantly impact imbalance reserve costs and storage resource participation: the 15-minute start time constraint, 30-minute ramp limit, 0.85 attenuation factor, and assumptions tying storage energy usage to reserve schedules. These design choices directly influence whether hydropower and pumped-storage resources can economically provide imbalance reserves and how their opportunity costs are calculated in optimization.

title: Presentation - Future Extended Day-Ahead Market (EDAM) Congestion Revenue Allocation - Mar 06, 2026
relevance_reason: EDAM congestion revenue allocation rules directly affect how hydropower and other dispatchable resources are incentivized to self-schedule and participate in day-ahead markets, with material implications for market efficiency and cost recovery.
stakeholders: Scott Harvey (California ISO Market Surveillance Committee)
ai_summary: The presentation analyzes loopflow pricing and congestion rent allocation mechanisms in EDAM, highlighting how self-scheduling incentives embedded in congestion charge rebate designs can create inefficiencies that disadvantage certain market participants (potentially including hydropower operators unable to readily self-schedule) and distort unit commitment and real-time dispatch outcomes. These market design choices affect the competitive positioning and cost recovery of flexible hydropower resources in CAISO's evolving market structure.

title: Presentation - Market Power Mitigation Issues - Market Surveillance Committee - Jan 16, 2026
relevance_reason: The presentation directly addresses market power mitigation issues and explicitly discusses how battery storage, pumped-storage hydropower, and energy-limited hydro resources should be treated in counterfactual market simulations and real-time dispatch analyses.
stakeholders: Scott Harvey (California ISO/Western EIM Market Surveillance Committee)
ai_summary: Scott Harvey argues that CAISO market power assessments must properly account for energy-limited resources (batteries, pondage hydro) in counterfactual simulations, noting that premature dispatch of these resources to reduce simulated prices is not evidence of market power exercise but rather operational necessity to meet load and avoid subsequent shortages. The presentation emphasizes that market power tests must accurately reflect how hydro and storage resources manage daily energy constraints.

title: Presentation - Price Formation Enhancements - Market Surveillance Committee - Jan 16, 2026
relevance_reason: The document addresses BAA-level market power mitigation, load-serving obligations, and scarcity pricing mechanisms that directly affect how hydropower and storage resources participate in CAISO/EDAM markets and are evaluated for withholdable capacity.
ai_summary: The Price Formation Enhancements initiative revises CAISO's market power mitigation test to incorporate load-serving obligations (LSO) when calculating withholdable capacity, which affects how hydropower and pumped-storage resources are assessed for competitiveness. Additionally, proposed scarcity pricing enhancements aim to improve price signals during tight supply conditions, which is critical for energy storage valuation and dispatch incentives.

title: Presentation - Price Formation Issues - Market Surveillance Committee - Jan 16, 2026
relevance_reason: The document directly addresses reserve products, price formation, and ancillary services that are critical to hydropower market participation, particularly flexible ramping and reserves procurement mechanisms.
stakeholders: Scott Harvey (California ISO / Western EIM Market Surveillance Committee)
ai_summary: The presentation discusses CAISO's reserve product design and proposes introducing explicit 30 or 60-minute reserve products to replace load conformance procurement. This is relevant to hydropower because hydro resources are typically called upon for flexible ramping and reserve services; changes to reserve product design, co-optimization with energy markets, and pricing mechanisms directly affect how hydro facilities are dispatched and compensated for providing these services.

title: Presentation - Scarcity Pricing - Market Surveillance Committee - Jan 16, 2026
relevance_reason: Scarcity pricing, penalty values, and reserve products directly affect how hydropower and storage resources are incentivized to retain energy and participate in CAISO/EDAM markets during tight conditions.
stakeholders: James Bushnell (California ISO / Western EIM Market Surveillance Committee)
ai_summary: The presentation discusses raising power balance constraint (PBC) penalty values from $1000/MWh to better reflect scarcity and incentivize storage resources (including pumped-storage hydropower) to retain energy for scarce periods. Higher scarcity penalties would strengthen incentives for storage dispatch flexibility and improve EDAM's ability to compete for scarce resources across the western market, directly impacting hydro participation and investment signals.

title: Presentation - Updated Analysis on Price Formation Enhancements - Market Surveillance Committee - Jan 16, 2026
relevance_reason: This document analyzes BAA market power mitigation (MPM) tests and price formation enhancements that directly affect how hydropower resources in balancing authority areas are evaluated for competitive supply adequacy and market participation.
ai_summary: CAISO is testing algorithmic refinements to its BAA-level market power mitigation framework, including grouping methods and Load Serving Obligation (LSO) components that affect how hydro and other dispatchable resources' capacity is counted in supply indices. The analysis impacts resource adequacy determinations and competitiveness assessments for multiple western BAAs including those with significant hydropower portfolios (e.g., PACW, SCL, PSEI, IPCO, NWMT).
